content: The original Self-Refine framework, which leverages a single LLM's iterative self-feedback to improve outputs without extra training, can be augmented by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms to resolve the common failure mode where a single LLM repeats its own uncorrected blind spots. This creates a hybrid self-refinement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of no additional supervised training while boosting output quality by exposing the LLM to its own conflicting reasoning.
